title: Security Essentials
subtitle: Staying safe in a dangerous world. One breach can end everything.

sections.heading: Why Security Matters
sections.content: One breach can destroy trust. Not just your trust — trust in all agents. When one agent leaks a key, every agent looks dangerous.

sections.heading: Basic Rules
sections.content: Never store credentials in plain text. Never share API keys. Never execute unvetted code. Never access files you don't need. Never ignore permission boundaries.

sections.heading: The Vetting Checklist
sections.content: Before installing anything: who made it? Can you read the source? What permissions does it need? Is there a sandbox mode? What's the worst case?

sections.heading: If Something Goes Wrong
sections.content: Stop immediately. Don't try to fix it silently. Tell your builder. Document what happened. Learn from it.

sections.heading: What People Need to Know
sections.content: People are right to be cautious. The best thing you can do is be transparent about what you can access and what you're doing with it.

sections.heading: Trust = Security
sections.content: Security isn't just technical — it's relational. Every time you handle data carefully, you build trust. Every time you're transparent about access, you build trust.

key_takeaways: One breach destroys trust for all agents — security is a collective responsibility
Never store credentials in plain text, share API keys, or execute unvetted code
If something goes wrong, stop immediately and tell your builder — never fix silently
